NKJV.1: At that time Jesus went through the grainfields on the Sabbath. And His disciples were hungry, and began to pluck heads of grain and to eat.
NKJV.2: And when the Pharisees saw it, they said to Him, “Look, Your disciples are doing what is not lawful to do on the Sabbath!”
NKJV.3: But He said to them, “Have you not read  what David did when he was hungry, he and those who were with him:
NKJV.4: how he entered the house of God and ate  the showbread which was not lawful for him to eat, nor for those who were with him,  but only for the priests?
NKJV.5: Or have you not read in the  law that on the Sabbath the priests in the temple profane the Sabbath, and are blameless?
NKJV.6: Yet I say to you that in this place there is One greater than the temple.
NKJV.7: But if you had known what this means, ‘I desire mercy and not sacrifice,’ you would not have condemned the guiltless.
NKJV.8: For the Son of Man is Lord even of the Sabbath.”
NKJV.9: Now when He had departed from there, He went into their synagogue.
NKJV.10: And behold, there was a man who had a withered hand. And they asked Him, saying, “Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?”—that they might accuse Him.
NKJV.11: Then He said to them, “What man is there among you who has one sheep, and if it falls into a pit on the Sabbath, will not lay hold of it and lift it out?
NKJV.12: Of how much more value then is a man than a sheep? Therefore it is lawful to do good on the Sabbath.”
NKJV.13: Then He said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” And he stretched it out, and it was restored as whole as the other.
NKJV.14: Then the Pharisees went out and plotted against Him, how they might destroy Him.
NKJV.15: But when Jesus knew it, He withdrew from there. And great multitudes followed Him, and He healed them all.
NKJV.16: Yet He warned them not to make Him known,
NKJV.17: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Isaiah the prophet, saying:
NKJV.18: “Behold! My Servant whom I have chosen, My Beloved in whom My soul is well pleased! I will put My Spirit upon Him, And He will declare justice to the Gentiles.
NKJV.19: He will not quarrel nor cry out, Nor will anyone hear His voice in the streets.
NKJV.20: A bruised reed He will not break, And smoking flax He will not quench, Till He sends forth justice to victory;
NKJV.21: And in His name Gentiles will trust.”
NKJV.22: Then one was brought to Him who was demon-possessed, blind and mute; and He healed him, so that the blind and mute man both spoke and saw.
NKJV.23: And all the multitudes were amazed and said, “Could this be the Son of David?”
NKJV.24: Now when the Pharisees heard it they said, “This fellow does not cast out demons except by Beelzebub, the ruler of the demons.”
NKJV.25: But Jesus knew their thoughts, and said to them: “Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation, and every city or house divided against itself will not stand.
NKJV.26: If Satan casts out Satan, he is divided against himself. How then will his kingdom stand?
NKJV.27: And if I cast out demons by Beelzebub, by whom do your sons cast them out? Therefore they shall be your judges.
NKJV.28: But if I cast out demons by the Spirit of God,  surely the kingdom of God has come upon you.
NKJV.29: Or how can one enter a strong man’s house and plunder his goods, unless he first binds the strong man? And then he will plunder his house.
NKJV.30: He who is not with Me is against Me, and he who does not gather with Me scatters abroad.
NKJV.31: “Therefore I say to you, every sin and blasphemy will be forgiven men, but the blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven men.
NKJV.32: Anyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man, it will be forgiven him; but whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit, it will not be forgiven him, either in this age or in the age to come.
NKJV.33: “Either make the tree good and its fruit good, or else make the tree bad and its fruit bad; for a tree is known by its fruit.
NKJV.34: Brood of vipers! How can you, being evil, speak good things?  For out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaks.
NKJV.35: A good man out of the good treasure of his heart brings forth good things, and an evil man out of the evil treasure brings forth evil things.
NKJV.36: But I say to you that for every idle word men may speak, they will give account of it in the day of judgment.
NKJV.37: For by your words you will be justified, and by your words you will be condemned.”
NKJV.38: Then some of the scribes and Pharisees answered, saying, “Teacher, we want to see a sign from You.”
NKJV.39: But He answered and said to them, “An evil and  adulterous generation seeks after a sign, and no sign will be given to it except the sign of the prophet Jonah.
NKJV.40: For as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the great fish, so will the Son of Man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.
NKJV.41: The men of Nineveh will rise up in the judgment with this generation and condemn it, because they repented at the preaching of Jonah; and indeed a greater than Jonah is here.
NKJV.42: The queen of the South will rise up in the judgment with this generation and condemn it, for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and indeed a greater than Solomon is here.
NKJV.43: “When an unclean spirit goes out of a man,  he goes through dry places, seeking rest, and finds none.
NKJV.44: Then he says, ‘I will return to my house from which I came.’ And when he comes, he finds it empty, swept, and put in order.
NKJV.45: Then he goes and takes with him seven other spirits more wicked than himself, and they enter and dwell there; and the last state of that man is worse than the first. So shall it also be with this wicked generation.”
NKJV.46: While He was still talking to the multitudes, behold, His mother and brothers stood outside, seeking to speak with Him.
NKJV.47: Then one said to Him, “Look, Your mother and Your brothers are standing outside, seeking to speak with You.”
NKJV.48: But He answered and said to the one who told Him, “Who is My mother and who are My brothers?”
NKJV.49: And He stretched out His hand toward His disciples and said, “Here are My mother and My  brothers!
NKJV.50: For  whoever does the will of My Father in heaven is My brother and sister and mother.”

ESV.1: At that time Jesus went through the grainfields on the Sabbath. His disciples were hungry, and they began to pluck heads of grain and to eat.
ESV.2: But when the Pharisees saw it, they said to him, “Look, your disciples are doing what is not lawful to do on the Sabbath.”
ESV.3: He said to them,  “Have you not read what David did when he was hungry, and those who were with him:
ESV.4: how he entered the house of God and ate the bread of the Presence, which it was not lawful for him to eat nor for those who were with him, but only for the priests?
ESV.5: Or have you not read in the Law how on the Sabbath the priests in the temple profane the Sabbath and are guiltless?
ESV.6: I tell you, something greater than the temple is here.
ESV.7: And if you had known what this means, ‘I desire mercy, and not sacrifice,’ you would not have condemned the guiltless.
ESV.8: For the Son of Man is lord of the Sabbath.”
ESV.9: He went on from there and entered their synagogue.
ESV.10: And a man was there with a withered hand. And they asked him, “Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?” — so that they might accuse him.
ESV.11: He said to them, “Which one of you who has a sheep, if it falls into a pit on the Sabbath, will not take hold of it and lift it out?
ESV.12: Of how much more value is a man than a sheep! So it is lawful to do good on the Sabbath.”
ESV.13: Then he said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” And the man stretched it out, and it was restored, healthy like the other.
ESV.14: But the Pharisees went out and conspired against him, how to destroy him.
ESV.15: Jesus, aware of this, withdrew from there. And many followed him, and he healed them all
ESV.16: and ordered them not to make him known.
ESV.17: This was to fulfill what was spoken by the prophet Isaiah:
ESV.18: “Behold, my servant whom I have chosen, my beloved with whom my soul is well pleased. I will put my Spirit upon him, and he will proclaim justice to the Gentiles.
ESV.19: He will not quarrel or cry aloud, nor will anyone hear his voice in the streets;
ESV.20: a bruised reed he will not break, and a smoldering wick he will not quench, until he brings justice to victory;
ESV.21: and in his name the Gentiles will hope.”
ESV.22: Then a demon-oppressed man who was blind and mute was brought to him, and he healed him, so that the man spoke and saw.
ESV.23: And all the people were amazed, and said, “Can this be the Son of David?”
ESV.24: But when the Pharisees heard it, they said, “It is only by Beelzebul, the prince of demons, that this man casts out demons.”
ESV.25: Knowing their thoughts, he said to them, “Every kingdom divided against itself is laid waste, and no city or house divided against itself will stand.
ESV.26: And if Satan casts out Satan, he is divided against himself. How then will his kingdom stand?
ESV.27: And if I cast out demons by Beelzebul, by whom do your sons cast them out? Therefore they will be your judges.
ESV.28: But if it is by the Spirit of God that I cast out demons, then the kingdom of God has come upon you.
ESV.29: Or how can someone enter a strong man's house and plunder his goods, unless he first binds the strong man? Then indeed he may plunder his house.
ESV.30: Whoever is not with me is against me, and whoever does not gather with me scatters.
ESV.31: Therefore I tell you, every sin and blasphemy will be forgiven people, but the blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven.
ESV.32: And whoever speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven, either in this age or in the age to come.
ESV.33: “Either make the tree good and its fruit good, or make the tree bad and its fruit bad, for the tree is known by its fruit.
ESV.34: You brood of vipers! How can you speak good, when you are evil? For out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaks.
ESV.35: The good person out of his good treasure brings forth good, and the evil person out of his evil treasure brings forth evil.
ESV.36: I tell you, on the day of judgment people will give account for every careless word they speak,
ESV.37: for by your words you will be justified, and by your words you will be condemned.”
ESV.38: Then some of the scribes and Pharisees answered him, saying, “Teacher, we wish to see a sign from you.”
ESV.39: But he answered them,  “An evil and adulterous generation seeks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except the sign of the prophet Jonah.
ESV.40: For just as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the great fish, so will the Son of Man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.
ESV.41: The men of Nineveh will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, for they repented at the preaching of Jonah, and behold, something greater than Jonah is here.
ESV.42: The queen of the South will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon, and behold, something greater than Solomon is here.
ESV.43: “When the unclean spirit has gone out of a person, it passes through waterless places seeking rest, but finds none.
ESV.44: Then it says, ‘I will return to my house from which I came.’ And when it comes, it finds the house empty, swept, and put in order.
ESV.45: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself, and they enter and dwell there, and the last state of that person is worse than the first. So also will it be with this evil generation.”
ESV.46: While he was still speaking to the people, behold, his mother and his brothers  stood outside, asking to speak to him.
ESV.48: But he replied to the man who told him, “Who is my mother, and who are my brothers?”
ESV.49: And stretching out his hand toward his disciples, he said, “Here are my mother and my brothers!
ESV.50: For whoever does the will of my Father in heaven is my brother and sister and mother.”

NASB.1: At that time Jesus went through the grainfields on the Sabbath, and His disciples became hungry and began to pick the heads [of grain] and eat.
NASB.2: But when the Pharisees saw [this], they said to Him, 'Look, Your disciples do what is not lawful to do on a Sabbath.'
NASB.3: But He said to them, 'Have you not read what David did when he became hungry, he and his companions,
NASB.4: how he entered the house of God, and they ate the consecrated bread, which was not lawful for him to eat nor for those with him, but for the priests alone?
NASB.5: 'Or have you not read in the Law, that on the Sabbath the priests in the temple break the Sabbath and are innocent?
NASB.6: 'But I say to you that something greater than the temple is here.
NASB.7: 'But if you had known what this means, 'I DESIRE COMPASSION, AND NOT A SACRIFICE,' you would not have condemned the innocent.
NASB.8: 'For the Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath.'
NASB.9: Departing from there, He went into their synagogue.
NASB.10: And a man [was there] whose hand was withered. And they questioned Jesus, asking, 'Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?'-- so that they might accuse Him.
NASB.11: And He said to them, 'What man is there among you who has a sheep, and if it falls into a pit on the Sabbath, will he not take hold of it and lift it out?
NASB.12: 'How much more valuable then is a man than a sheep! So then, it is lawful to do good on the Sabbath.'
NASB.13: Then He said to the man, 'Stretch out your hand!' He stretched it out, and it was restored to normal, like the other.
NASB.14: But the Pharisees went out and conspired against Him, [as to] how they might destroy Him.
NASB.15: But Jesus, aware of [this], withdrew from there. Many followed Him, and He healed them all,
NASB.16: and warned them not to tell who He was.
NASB.17: [This was] to fulfill what was spoken through Isaiah the prophet:
NASB.18: 'BEHOLD, MY SERVANT WHOM I HAVE CHOSEN; MY BELOVED IN WHOM MY SOUL is WELL-PLEASED; I WILL PUT MY SPIRIT UPON HIM, AND HE SHALL PROCLAIM JUSTICE TO THE GENTILES.
NASB.19: 'HE WILL NOT QUARREL, NOR CRY OUT; NOR WILL ANYONE HEAR HIS VOICE IN THE STREETS.
NASB.20: 'A BATTERED REED HE WILL NOT BREAK OFF, AND A SMOLDERING WICK HE WILL NOT PUT OUT, UNTIL HE LEADS JUSTICE TO VICTORY.
NASB.21: 'AND IN HIS NAME THE GENTILES WILL HOPE.'
NASB.22: Then a demon-possessed man [who was] blind and mute was brought to Jesus, and He healed him, so that the mute man spoke and saw.
NASB.23: All the crowds were amazed, and were saying, 'This man cannot be the Son of David, can he?'
NASB.24: But when the Pharisees heard [this], they said, 'This man casts out demons only by Beelzebul the ruler of the demons.'
NASB.25: And knowing their thoughts Jesus said to them, 'Any kingdom divided against itself is laid waste; and any city or house divided against itself will not stand.
NASB.26: 'If Satan casts out Satan, he is divided against himself; how then will his kingdom stand?
NASB.27: 'If I by Beelzebul cast out demons, by whom do your sons cast [them] out? For this reason they will be your judges.
NASB.28: 'But if I cast out demons by the Spirit of God, then the kingdom of God has come upon you.
NASB.29: 'Or how can anyone enter the strong man's house and carry off his property, unless he first binds the strong [man]? And then he will plunder his house.
NASB.30: 'He who is not with Me is against Me; and he who does not gather with Me scatters.
NASB.31: 'Therefore I say to you, any sin and blasphemy shall be forgiven people, but blasphemy against the Spirit shall not be forgiven.
NASB.32: 'Whoever speaks a word against the Son of Man, it shall be forgiven him; but whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit, it shall not be forgiven him, either in this age or in the [age] to come.
NASB.33: 'Either make the tree good and its fruit good, or make the tree bad and its fruit bad; for the tree is known by its fruit.
NASB.34: 'You brood of vipers, how can you, being evil, speak what is good? For the mouth speaks out of that which fills the heart.
NASB.35: 'The good man brings out of [his] good treasure what is good; and the evil man brings out of [his] evil treasure what is evil.
NASB.36: 'But I tell you that every careless word that people speak, they shall give an accounting for it in the day of judgment.
NASB.37: 'For by your words you will be justified, and by your words you will be condemned.'
NASB.38: Then some of the scribes and Pharisees said to Him, 'Teacher, we want to see a sign from You.'
NASB.39: But He answered and said to them, 'An evil and adulterous generation craves for a sign; and [yet] no sign will be given to it but the sign of Jonah the prophet;
NASB.40: for just as JONAH WAS THREE DAYS AND THREE NIGHTS IN THE BELLY OF THE SEA MONSTER, so will the Son of Man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.
NASB.41: 'The men of Nineveh will stand up with this generation at the judgment, and will condemn it because they repented at the preaching of Jonah; and behold, something greater than Jonah is here.
NASB.42: '[The] Queen of [the] South will rise up with this generation at the judgment and will condemn it, because she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and behold, something greater than Solomon is here.
NASB.43: 'Now when the unclean spirit goes out of a man, it passes through waterless places seeking rest, and does not find [it].
NASB.44: 'Then it says, 'I will return to my house from which I came'; and when it comes, it finds [it] unoccupied, swept, and put in order.
NASB.45: 'Then it goes and takes along with it seven other spirits more wicked than itself, and they go in and live there; and the last state of that man becomes worse than the first. That is the way it will also be with this evil generation.'
NASB.46: While He was still speaking to the crowds, behold, His mother and brothers were standing outside, seeking to speak to Him.
NASB.47: Someone said to Him, 'Behold, Your mother and Your brothers are standing outside seeking to speak to You.'
NASB.48: But Jesus answered the one who was telling Him and said, 'Who is My mother and who are My brothers?'
NASB.49: And stretching out His hand toward His disciples, He said, 'Behold My mother and My brothers!
NASB.50: 'For whoever does the will of My Father who is in heaven, he is My brother and sister and mother.'

NLT.1: At about that time Jesus was walking through some grainfields on the Sabbath. His disciples were hungry, so they began breaking off some heads of grain and eating them.
NLT.2: But some Pharisees saw them do it and protested, “Look, your disciples are breaking the law by harvesting grain on the Sabbath.”
NLT.3: Jesus said to them, “Haven’t you read in the Scriptures what David did when he and his companions were hungry?
NLT.4: He went into the house of God, and he and his companions broke the law by eating the sacred loaves of bread that only the priests are allowed to eat.
NLT.5: And haven’t you read in the law of Moses that the priests on duty in the Temple may work on the Sabbath?
NLT.6: I tell you, there is one here who is even greater than the Temple!
NLT.7: But you would not have condemned my innocent disciples if you knew the meaning of this Scripture: ‘I want you to show mercy, not offer sacrifices.’
NLT.8: For the Son of Man is Lord, even over the Sabbath!”
NLT.9: Then Jesus went over to their synagogue,
NLT.10: where he noticed a man with a deformed hand. The Pharisees asked Jesus, “Does the law permit a person to work by healing on the Sabbath?” (They were hoping he would say yes, so they could bring charges against him.)
NLT.11: And he answered, “If you had a sheep that fell into a well on the Sabbath, wouldn’t you work to pull it out? Of course you would.
NLT.12: And how much more valuable is a person than a sheep! Yes, the law permits a person to do good on the Sabbath.”
NLT.13: Then he said to the man, “Hold out your hand.” So the man held out his hand, and it was restored, just like the other one!
NLT.14: Then the Pharisees called a meeting to plot how to kill Jesus.
NLT.15: But Jesus knew what they were planning. So he left that area, and many people followed him. He healed all the sick among them,
NLT.16: but he warned them not to reveal who he was.
NLT.17: This fulfilled the prophecy of Isaiah concerning him:
NLT.18:
“Look at my Servant, whom I have chosen.
He is my Beloved, who pleases me.
I will put my Spirit upon him,
and he will proclaim justice to the nations.
NLT.19:
He will not fight or shout
or raise his voice in public.
NLT.20:
He will not crush the weakest reed
or put out a flickering candle.
Finally he will cause justice to be victorious.
NLT.21:
And his name will be the hope
of all the world.”
NLT.22: Then a demon-possessed man, who was blind and couldn’t speak, was brought to Jesus. He healed the man so that he could both speak and see.
NLT.23: The crowd was amazed and asked, “Could it be that Jesus is the Son of David, the Messiah?”
NLT.24: But when the Pharisees heard about the miracle, they said, “No wonder he can cast out demons. He gets his power from Satan, the prince of demons.”
NLT.25: Jesus knew their thoughts and replied, “Any kingdom divided by civil war is doomed. A town or family splintered by feuding will fall apart.
NLT.26: And if Satan is casting out Satan, he is divided and fighting against himself. His own kingdom will not survive.
NLT.27: And if I am empowered by Satan, what about your own exorcists? They cast out demons, too, so they will condemn you for what you have said.
NLT.28: But if I am casting out demons by the Spirit of God, then the Kingdom of God has arrived among you.
NLT.29: For who is powerful enough to enter the house of a strong man and plunder his goods? Only someone even stronger — someone who could tie him up and then plunder his house.
NLT.30: “Anyone who isn’t with me opposes me, and anyone who isn’t working with me is actually working against me.
NLT.31: “So I tell you, every sin and blasphemy can be forgiven — except blasphemy against the Holy Spirit, which will never be forgiven.
NLT.32: Anyone who speaks against the Son of Man can be forgiven, but anyone who speaks against the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven, either in this world or in the world to come.
NLT.33: “A tree is identified by its fruit. If a tree is good, its fruit will be good. If a tree is bad, its fruit will be bad.
NLT.34: You brood of snakes! How could evil men like you speak what is good and right? For whatever is in your heart determines what you say.
NLT.35: A good person produces good things from the treasury of a good heart, and an evil person produces evil things from the treasury of an evil heart.
NLT.36: And I tell you this, you must give an account on judgment day for every idle word you speak.
NLT.37: The words you say will either acquit you or condemn you.”
NLT.38: One day some teachers of religious law and Pharisees came to Jesus and said, “Teacher, we want you to show us a miraculous sign to prove your authority.”
NLT.39: But Jesus replied, “Only an evil, adulterous generation would demand a miraculous sign; but the only sign I will give them is the sign of the prophet Jonah.
NLT.40: For as Jonah was in the belly of the great fish for three days and three nights, so will the Son of Man be in the heart of the earth for three days and three nights.
NLT.41: “The people of Nineveh will stand up against this generation on judgment day and condemn it, for they repented of their sins at the preaching of Jonah. Now someone greater than Jonah is here — but you refuse to repent.
NLT.42: The queen of Sheba will also stand up against this generation on judgment day and condemn it, for she came from a distant land to hear the wisdom of Solomon. Now someone greater than Solomon is here — but you refuse to listen.
NLT.43: “When an evil spirit leaves a person, it goes into the desert, seeking rest but finding none.
NLT.44: Then it says, ‘I will return to the person I came from.’ So it returns and finds its former home empty, swept, and in order.
NLT.45: Then the spirit finds seven other spirits more evil than itself, and they all enter the person and live there. And so that person is worse off than before. That will be the experience of this evil generation.”
NLT.46: As Jesus was speaking to the crowd, his mother and brothers stood outside, asking to speak to him.
NLT.47: Someone told Jesus, “Your mother and your brothers are standing outside, and they want to speak to you.”
NLT.48: Jesus asked, “Who is my mother? Who are my brothers?”
NLT.49: Then he pointed to his disciples and said, “Look, these are my mother and brothers.
NLT.50: Anyone who does the will of my Father in heaven is my brother and sister and mother!”

WEB.1: At that time, Jesus went on the Sabbath day through the grain fields. His disciples were hungry and began to pluck heads of grain and to eat.
WEB.2: But the Pharisees, when they saw it, said to him, “Behold, your disciples do what is not lawful to do on the Sabbath.”
WEB.3: But he said to them, “Haven’t you read what David did, when he was hungry, and those who were with him;
WEB.4: how he entered into God’s house, and ate the show bread, which was not lawful for him to eat, neither for those who were with him, but only for the priests?
WEB.5: Or have you not read in the law, that on the Sabbath day, the priests in the temple profane the Sabbath, and are guiltless?
WEB.6: But I tell you that one greater than the temple is here.
WEB.7: But if you had known what this means, ‘I desire mercy, and not sacrifice,’  you would not have condemned the guiltless.
WEB.8: For the Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath.”
WEB.9: He departed there, and went into their synagogue.
WEB.10: And behold there was a man with a withered hand. They asked him, “Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath day?” that they might accuse him.
WEB.11: He said to them, “What man is there among you, who has one sheep, and if this one falls into a pit on the Sabbath day, won’t he grab on to it, and lift it out?
WEB.12: Of how much more value then is a man than a sheep! Therefore it is lawful to do good on the Sabbath day.”
WEB.13: Then he told the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He stretched it out; and it was restored whole, just like the other.
WEB.14: But the Pharisees went out, and conspired against him, how they might destroy him.
WEB.15: Jesus, perceiving that, withdrew from there. Great multitudes followed him; and he healed them all,
WEB.16: and commanded them that they should not make him known:
WEB.17: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken through Isaiah the prophet, saying,
WEB.18: “Behold, my servant whom I have chosen; my beloved in whom my soul is well pleased: I will put my Spirit on him. He will proclaim justice to the nations.
WEB.19: He will not strive, nor shout; neither will anyone hear his voice in the streets.
WEB.20: He won’t break a bruised reed. He won’t quench a smoking flax, until he leads justice to victory.
WEB.21: In his name, the nations will hope.”
WEB.22: Then one possessed by a demon, blind and mute, was brought to him and he healed him, so that the blind and mute man both spoke and saw.
WEB.23: All the multitudes were amazed, and said, “Can this be the son of David?”
WEB.24: But when the Pharisees heard it, they said, “This man does not cast out demons, except by Beelzebul, the prince of the demons.”
WEB.25: Knowing their thoughts, Jesus said to them, “Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation, and every city or house divided against itself will not stand.
WEB.26: If Satan casts out Satan, he is divided against himself. How then will his kingdom stand?
WEB.27: If I by Beelzebul cast out demons, by whom do your children cast them out? Therefore they will be your judges.
WEB.28: But if I by the Spirit of God cast out demons, then God’s Kingdom has come upon you.

CSB17.1: At that time Jesus passed through the grainfields on the Sabbath. <sup>ⓚ</sup> His disciples <sup>ⓛ</sup> were hungry and began to pick and eat some heads of grain.

CSB17.3: He said to them, “Haven’t you read what David did when he and those who were with him were hungry:
CSB17.4: how he entered the house of God, and they ate <sup>[1]</sup>the bread of the Presence — which is not lawful for him or for those with him to eat, but only for the priests? <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.5: Or haven’t you read in the law that on Sabbath days the priests in the temple violate the Sabbath and are innocent? <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.6: I tell you that something greater than the temple is here. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.7: If you had known what this means, I desire mercy and not sacrifice, [2] ⓒyou would not have condemned the innocent.
CSB17.8: For the Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath.” <sup>ⓓ</sup>

CSB17.12: A person is worth far more than a sheep;  <sup>ⓗ</sup>so it is lawful to do what is good on the Sabbath.”
CSB17.13: Then he told the man, “Stretch out your hand.” So he stretched it out, and it was restored, <sup>ⓘ</sup> as good as the other.

AMP.1: At that particular time Jesus went through the grainfields on the Sabbath, and His disciples were hungry and began to pick the heads of grain and eat them.
AMP.2: But when the Pharisees saw this, they said to Him, "Look! Your disciples are doing what is unlawful on the Sabbath."
AMP.3: He said to them, "Have you not read [in the Scriptures] what David did when he was hungry, he and those who accompanied him—
AMP.4: how he went into the house of God, and they ate the consecrated bread, which was not lawful for him to eat nor for those with him, but for the priests only?
AMP.5: Or have you not read in the Law, that on the Sabbath the priests in the temple break [the sanctity of] the Sabbath and yet are innocent?
AMP.6: But I tell you that something greater than the temple is here.
AMP.7: And if you had only known what this statement means, 'I desire compassion [for those in distress],and not [animal] sacrifice,' you would not have condemned the innocent.
AMP.8: For the Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath."
AMP.9: Leaving there, He went into their synagogue.
AMP.10: A man was there whose hand was withered. And they asked Jesus, "Is it lawful and permissible to heal on the Sabbath?"—they asked this so that they might accuse Him and bring charges into court.
AMP.11: But He said to them, "What man is there among you who, if he has only one sheep and it falls into a pit on the Sabbath, will not take hold of it and lift it out?
AMP.12: How much more valuable then is a man than a sheep! So it is lawful and permissible to do good on the Sabbath."
AMP.13: Then He said to the man, "Reach out your hand!" The man reached out and it was restored, as normal and healthy as the other.
AMP.14: But the Pharisees went out and conspired against Him, discussing how they could destroy Him.
AMP.15: Being aware of this, Jesus left there. Many followed Him, and He healed all of them [who were sick],
AMP.16: and warned them not to tell [publicly] who He was.
AMP.17: This was to fulfill what was spoken by the prophet Isaiah:
AMP.18:
"Behold, My Servant whom I have chosen;
My Beloved in whom My soul is well-pleased;
I will put My Spirit upon Him,
And He will proclaim justice to the nations.
AMP.19:
"He will not quarrel, nor cry out loudly;
Nor will anyone hear His voice in the streets.
AMP.20:
"A battered reed He will not break,
And a smoldering wick He will not extinguish,
Until He leads justice to victory.
AMP.21: "And in His name the Gentiles (all the nations of the world) will hope [with confidence]."
AMP.22: Then a demon-possessed man who was blind and mute was brought to Jesus, and He healed him, so that the mute man both spoke and saw.
AMP.23: All the people wondered in amazement, and said, "Could this be the Son of David (the Messiah)?"
AMP.24: But the Pharisees heard it and said, "This man casts out demons only by [the help of] Beelzebul (Satan) the prince of the demons."
AMP.25: Knowing their thoughts Jesus said to them, "Any kingdom that is divided against itself is being laid waste; and no city or house divided against itself will [continue to] stand.
AMP.26: If Satan casts out Satan [that is, his demons], he has become divided against himself and disunited; how then will his kingdom stand?
AMP.27: If I cast out the demons by [the help of] Beelzebul (Satan), by whom do your sons drive them out? For this reason they will be your judges.
AMP.28: But if it is by the Spirit of God that I cast out the demons, then the kingdom of God has come upon you [before you expected it].
AMP.29: Or how can anyone go into a strong man's house and steal his property unless he first overpowers and ties up the strong man? Then he will ransack and rob his house.
AMP.30: He who is not with Me [once and for all on My side] is against Me; and he who does not [unequivocally] gather with Me scatters.
AMP.31: "Therefore I say to you, every sin and blasphemy [every evil, abusive, injurious speaking, or indignity against sacred things] will be forgiven people, but blasphemy against the [Holy] Spirit will not be forgiven.
AMP.32: Whoever speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven; but whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit [by attributing the miracles done by Me to Satan] will not be forgiven, either in this age or in the age to come.
AMP.33: "Either make the tree good and its fruit good, or make the tree bad and its fruit bad; for the tree is recognized and judged by its fruit.
AMP.34: You brood of vipers, how can you speak good things when you are evil? For the mouth speaks out of that which fills the heart.
AMP.35: The good man, from his [inner] good treasure, brings out good things; and the evil man, from his [inner] evil treasure, brings out evil things.
AMP.36: But I tell you, on the day of judgment people will have to give an accounting for every careless or useless word they speak.
AMP.37: For by your words [reflecting your spiritual condition] you will be justified and acquitted of the guilt of sin; and by your words [rejecting Me] you will be condemned and sentenced."
AMP.38: Then some of the scribes and Pharisees said to Him, "Teacher, we want to see a sign (attesting miracle) from You [proving that You are what You claim to be]."
AMP.39: But He replied and said to them, "An evil and adulterous generation [that is morally unfaithful to God] craves and demands a [miraculous] sign; but no sign will be given to it except the sign of the prophet Jonah;
AMP.40: for just as <e>Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the sea monster</e>, so will the Son of Man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.
AMP.41: The men of Nineveh will stand up [as witnesses] at the judgment against this generation, and will condemn it because they repented at the preaching of Jonah; and now, something greater than Jonah is here.
AMP.42: The Queen of the South (Sheba) will stand up [as a witness] at the judgment against this generation, and will condemn it because she came from the ends of the earth to listen to the wisdom of Solomon; and now, something greater than Solomon is here.
AMP.43: "Now when the unclean spirit has gone out of a man, it roams through waterless (dry, arid) places in search of rest, but it does not find it.
AMP.44: Then it says, 'I will return to my house from which I came.' And when it arrives, it finds the place unoccupied, swept, and put in order.
AMP.45: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more wicked than itself, and they go in and make their home there. And the last condition of that man becomes worse than the first. So will it also be with this wicked generation."
AMP.46: While He was still talking to the crowds, it happened that His mother and brothers stood outside, asking to speak to Him.
AMP.47: Someone said to Him, "Look! Your mother and Your brothers are standing outside asking to speak to You."
AMP.48: But Jesus replied to the one who told Him, "Who is My mother and who are My brothers?"
AMP.49: And stretching out His hand toward His disciples [and all His other followers], He said, "Here are My mother and My brothers!
AMP.50: For whoever does the will of My Father who is in heaven [by believing in Me, and following Me] is My brother and sister and mother."

MSG.1: One Sabbath, Jesus was strolling with his disciples through a field of ripe grain. Hungry, the disciples were pulling off the heads of grain and munching on them.
MSG.2: Some Pharisees reported them to Jesus: "Your disciples are breaking the Sabbath rules!"
MSG.3: Jesus said, "Really? Didn't you ever read what David and his companions did when they were hungry,
MSG.4: how they entered the sanctuary and ate fresh bread off the altar, bread that no one but priests were allowed to eat?
MSG.5: And didn't you ever read in God's Law that priests carrying out their Temple duties break Sabbath rules all the time and it's not held against them?
MSG.6: "There is far more at stake here than religion.
MSG.7: If you had any idea what this Scripture meant—'I prefer a flexible heart to an inflexible ritual'—you wouldn't be nitpicking like this.
MSG.8: The Son of Man is no lackey to the Sabbath; he's in charge."
MSG.9: When Jesus left the field, he entered their meeting place.
MSG.10: There was a man there with a crippled hand. They said to Jesus, "Is it legal to heal on the Sabbath?" They were baiting him.
MSG.11: He replied, "Is there a person here who, finding one of your lambs fallen into a ravine, wouldn't, even though it was a Sabbath, pull it out?
MSG.12: Surely kindness to people is as legal as kindness to animals!"
MSG.13: Then he said to the man, "Hold out your hand." He held it out and it was healed.
MSG.14: The Pharisees walked out furious, sputtering about how they were going to ruin Jesus.
MSG.15: Jesus, knowing they were out to get him, moved on. A lot of people followed him, and he healed them all
MSG.16: . He also cautioned them to keep it quiet,
MSG.17: following guidelines set down by Isaiah:
MSG.18: Look well at my handpicked servant; I love him so much, take such delight in him. I've placed my Spirit on him; he'll decree justice to the nations.
MSG.19: But he won't yell, won't raise his voice; there'll be no commotion in the streets.
MSG.20: He won't walk over anyone's feelings, won't push you into a corner. Before you know it, his justice will triumph;
MSG.21: the mere sound of his name will signal hope, even among far-off unbelievers.
MSG.22: Next a poor demon-afflicted wretch, both blind and deaf, was set down before him. Jesus healed him, gave him his sight and hearing.
MSG.23: The people who saw it were impressed—"This has to be the Son of David!"
MSG.24: But the Pharisees, when they heard the report, were cynical. "Black magic," they said. "Some devil trick he's pulled from his sleeve."
MSG.25: Jesus confronted their slander. "A judge who gives opposite verdicts on the same person cancels himself out; a family that's in a constant squabble disintegrates;
MSG.26: if Satan banishes Satan, is there any Satan left?
MSG.27: If you're slinging devil mud at me, calling me a devil kicking out devils, doesn't the same mud stick to your own exorcists?
MSG.28: "But if it's by God's power that I am sending the evil spirits packing, then God's kingdom is here for sure.
MSG.29: How in the world do you think it's possible in broad daylight to enter the house of an awake, able-bodied man and walk off with his possessions unless you tie him up first? Tie him up, though, and you can clean him out.
MSG.30: "This is war, and there is no neutral ground. If you're not on my side, you're the enemy; if you're not helping, you're making things worse.
MSG.31: "There's nothing done or said that can't be forgiven. But if you deliberately persist in your slanders against God's Spirit, you are repudiating the very One who forgives.
MSG.32: If you reject the Son of Man out of some misunderstanding, the Holy Spirit can forgive you, but when you reject the Holy Spirit, you're sawing off the branch on which you're sitting, severing by your own perversity all connection with the One who forgives.
MSG.33: "If you grow a healthy tree, you'll pick healthy fruit. If you grow a diseased tree, you'll pick worm-eaten fruit. The fruit tells you about the tree.
MSG.34: "You have minds like a snake pit! How do you suppose what you say is worth anything when you are so foul-minded? It's your heart, not the dictionary, that gives meaning to your words.
MSG.35: A good person produces good deeds and words season after season. An evil person is a blight on the orchard.
MSG.36: Let me tell you something: Every one of these careless words is going to come back to haunt you. There will be a time of Reckoning. Words are powerful; take them seriously.
MSG.37: Words can be your salvation. Words can also be your damnation."
MSG.38: Later a few religion scholars and Pharisees got on him. "Teacher, we want to see your credentials. Give us some hard evidence that God is in this. How about a miracle?"
MSG.39: Jesus said, "You're looking for proof, but you're looking for the wrong kind. All you want is something to titillate your curiosity, satisfy your lust for miracles. The only proof you're going to get is what looks like the absence of proof: Jonah-evidence.
MSG.40: Like Jonah, three days and nights in the fish's belly, the Son of Man will be gone three days and nights in a deep grave.
MSG.41: "On Judgment Day, the Ninevites will stand up and give evidence that will condemn this generation, because when Jonah preached to them they changed their lives. A far greater preacher than Jonah is here, and you squabble about 'proofs.'
MSG.42: On Judgment Day, the Queen of Sheba will come forward and bring evidence that will condemn this generation, because she traveled from a far corner of the earth to listen to wise Solomon. Wisdom far greater than Solomon's is right in front of you, and you quibble over 'evidence.'
MSG.43: "When a defiling evil spirit is expelled from someone, it drifts along through the desert looking for an oasis, some unsuspecting soul it can bedevil. When it doesn't find anyone,
MSG.44: it says, 'I'll go back to my old haunt.' On return it finds the person spotlessly clean, but vacant.
MSG.45: It then runs out and rounds up seven other spirits more evil than itself and they all move in, whooping it up. That person ends up far worse off than if he'd never gotten cleaned up in the first place. "That's what this generation is like: You may think you have cleaned out the junk from your lives and gotten ready for God, but you weren't hospitable to my kingdom message, and now all the devils are moving back in."
MSG.46: While he was still talking to the crowd, his mother and brothers showed up. They were outside trying to get a message to him.
MSG.47: Someone told Jesus, "Your mother and brothers are out here, wanting to speak with you."
MSG.48: Jesus didn't respond directly, but said, "Who do you think my mother and brothers are?"
MSG.49: He then stretched out his hand toward his disciples. "Look closely. These are my mother and brothers.
MSG.50: Obedience is thicker than blood. The person who obeys my heavenly Father's will is my brother and sister and mother."

NIV.36: But I tell you that men will have to give account on the day of judgment for every careless word they have spoken.
NIV.37: For by your words you will be acquitted, and by your words you will be condemned.”
NIV.38:
The Sign of Jonah
Then some of the Pharisees and teachers of the law said to him, “Teacher, we want to see a miraculous sign from you.”
NIV.39: He answered, “A wicked and adulterous generation asks for a miraculous sign! But none will be given it except the sign of the prophet Jonah.
NIV.40: For as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of a huge fish, so the Son of Man will be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.
NIV.41: The men of Nineveh will stand up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it; for they repented at the preaching of Jonah, and now one greater than Jonah is here.
NIV.42: The Queen of the South will rise at the judgment with this generation and condemn it; for she came from the ends of the earth to listen to Solomon’s wisdom, and now one greater than Solomon is here.
NIV.43: “When an evil spirit comes out of a man, it goes through arid places seeking rest and does not find it.
NIV.44: Then it says, ‘I will return to the house I left.’ When it arrives, it finds the house unoccupied, swept clean and put in order.
NIV.45: Then it goes and takes with it seven other spirits more wicked than itself, and they go in and live there. And the final condition of that man is worse than the first. That is how it will be with this wicked generation.”
NIV.46:
Jesus’ Mother and Brothers
While Jesus was still talking to the crowd, his mother and brothers stood outside, wanting to speak to him.
NIV.47: Someone told him, “Your mother and brothers are standing outside, wanting to speak to you.”
NIV.48: He replied to him, “Who is my mother, and who are my brothers?”
NIV.49: Pointing to his disciples, he said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.
NIV.50: For whoever does the will of my Father in heaven is my brother and sister and mother.”

NET.1: At that time Jesus went through the grain fields on a Sabbath. His disciples were hungry, and they began to pick heads of wheat and eat them.
NET.2: But when the Pharisees saw this they said to him, “Look, your disciples are doing what is against the law to do on the Sabbath.”
NET.3: He said to them, “Haven’t you read what David did when he and his companions were hungry —
NET.4: how he entered the house of God and they ate the sacred bread, which was against the law for him or his companions to eat, but only for the priests?
NET.5: Or have you not read in the law that the priests in the temple desecrate the Sabbath and yet are not guilty?
NET.6: I tell you that something greater than the temple is here.
NET.7: If you had known what this means: ‘I want mercy and not sacrifice,’ you would not have condemned the innocent.
NET.8: For the Son of Man is lord of the Sabbath.”
NET.9: Then Jesus left that place and entered their synagogue.
NET.10: A man was there who had a withered hand. And they asked Jesus, “Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?” so that they could accuse him.
NET.11: He said to them, “Would not any one of you, if he had one sheep that fell into a pit on the Sabbath, take hold of it and lift it out?
NET.12: How much more valuable is a person than a sheep! So it is lawful to do good on the Sabbath.”
NET.13: Then he said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He stretched it out and it was restored, as healthy as the other.
NET.14: But the Pharisees went out and plotted against him, as to how they could assassinate him.
NET.15: Now when Jesus learned of this, he went away from there. Great crowds followed him, and he healed them all.
NET.16: But he sternly warned them not to make him known.
NET.17: This fulfilled what was spoken by Isaiah the prophet:
NET.18: “Here is my servant whom I have chosen, the one I love, in whom I take great delight. I will put my Spirit on him, and he will proclaim justice to the nations.
NET.19: He will not quarrel or cry out, nor will anyone hear his voice in the streets.
NET.20: He will not break a bruised reed or extinguish a smoldering wick, until he brings justice to victory.
NET.21: And in his name the Gentiles will hope.”
NET.22: Then they brought to him a demon-possessed man who was blind and mute. Jesus healed him so that he could speak and see.
NET.23: All the crowds were amazed and said, “Could this one be the Son of David?”
NET.24: But when the Pharisees heard this they said, “He does not cast out demons except by the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons!”
NET.25: Now when Jesus realized what they were thinking, he said to them, “Every kingdom divided against itself is destroyed, and no town or house divided against itself will stand.
NET.26: So if Satan casts out Satan, he is divided against himself. How then will his kingdom stand?
NET.27: And if I cast out demons by Beelzebul, by whom do your sons cast them out? For this reason they will be your judges.
NET.28: But if I cast out demons by the Spirit of God, then the kingdom of God has already overtaken you.
NET.29: How else can someone enter a strong man’s house and steal his property, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can thoroughly plunder the house.
NET.30: Whoever is not with me is against me, and whoever does not gather with me scatters.
NET.31: For this reason I tell you, people will be forgiven for every sin and blasphemy, but the blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven.
NET.32: Whoever speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven. But whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven, either in this age or in the age to come.
NET.33: “Make a tree good and its fruit will be good, or make a tree bad and its fruit will be bad, for a tree is known by its fruit.
NET.34: Offspring of vipers! How are you able to say anything good, since you are evil? For the mouth speaks from what fills the heart.
NET.35: The good person brings good things out of his good treasury, and the evil person brings evil things out of his evil treasury.
NET.36: I tell you that on the day of judgment, people will give an account for every worthless word they speak.
NET.37: For by your words you will be justified, and by your words you will be condemned.”
NET.38: Then some of the experts in the law along with some Pharisees answered him, “Teacher, we want to see a sign from you.”
NET.39: But he answered them, “An evil and adulterous generation asks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except the sign of the prophet Jonah.
NET.40: For just as Jonah was in the belly of the huge fish for three days and three nights, so the Son of Man will be in the heart of the earth for three days and three nights.
NET.41: The people of Nineveh will stand up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, because they repented when Jonah preached to them — and now, something greater than Jonah is here!
NET.42: The queen of the South will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, because she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon — and now, something greater than Solomon is here!
NET.43: “When an unclean spirit goes out of a person, it passes through waterless places looking for rest but does not find it.
NET.44: Then it says, ‘I will return to the home I left.’ When it returns, it finds the house empty, swept clean, and put in order.
NET.45: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself, and they go in and live there, so the last state of that person is worse than the first. It will be that way for this evil generation as well!”
NET.46: While Jesus was still speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers came and stood outside, asking to speak to him.
NET.47: Someone told him, “Look, your mother and your brothers are standing outside wanting to speak to you.”
NET.48: To the one who had said this, Jesus replied, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
NET.49: And pointing toward his disciples he said, “Here are my mother and my brothers!
NET.50: For whoever does the will of my Father in heaven is my brother and sister and mother.”
